We met a man at a citywide garage sale with a hand-built cardboard PERISCOPE, a sunglass piñata, and a story about Catholic boarding school in Italy — and that's just the first three minutes.

In this 33-minute Shed Flips vlog Lonnie and Candice wrap up the last of the Abita Springs citywide sale with one of the more memorable characters they've met (the cardboard periscope inventor), then come home and pull/pack a heavy order day. Highlights: a Wilton Roman column cake separator set picked up for $12.50 at an estate sale that sold for $100 within hours of listing, a 1991 Shrimp Festival mug that pays for the whole 5-mug lot, a Boston Marathon Procreations poster restoration session ($5 buy, listed $150), a 150-piece vintage car brochure lot from the 70s/80s/90s, and the Shed Flops segment where Lonnie discovers his Isabella Fiore purse listing is missing the word "purse" in the title.
